title: davallia polypodioides var. strigosa
intro: Davallia polypodioides var. strigosa, also known as the hairy davallia fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Davalliaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its rhizomes can be used to make a tincture or extract for medicinal purposes.
